text.title: Kubernetes ResourceQuota Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules plus a skip-clause for `Kubernetes ResourceQuota`. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: ResourceQuota MUST be paired with a LimitRange in the same namespace; ResourceQuota alone is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without LimitRange, pods may admit without explicit requests and ResourceQuota cannot enforce.

rule.statement: Production quotas MUST cap both compute (CPU + memory) AND storage (requests.storage, persistentvolumeclaims).
rule.rationale: Storage explosion is the second-most-common cluster-cost surprise after compute.

rule.statement: Production quotas MUST cap key object counts (pods, services, configmaps, secrets, persistentvolumeclaims).
rule.rationale: Without object caps, runaway loops can create millions of objects and crash etcd.

rule.statement: If using priority classes, quotas MUST use scopeSelector to separate priorityClass critical from priorityClass best-effort.
rule.rationale: Without scope, low-priority workloads can exhaust the quota and starve critical pods.

rule.statement: Each ResourceQuota MUST have a named tenant owner who can negotiate increases; anonymous quotas are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Anonymous quotas rot; named owner forces review on growth.

rule.statement: If Applies If preconditions fail OR any Skip If trigger fires, do not run this methodology.
